question_text: Let G = (V, E) be a directed, weighted graph with weight function w : E → R. For some function f : V → R, for each edge (u, v) ∈ E, define w′(u, v) as w(u, v) + f(v). Which one of the options completes the following sentence so that it is TRUE? “The shortest paths in G under w are shortest paths under w′ too, ________”.

options.A: if and only if f(u) is the distance from s to u in the graph obtained by adding a new vertex s to G and edges of zero weight from s to every vertex of G
options.B: if and only if ∀u ∈ V, f(u) is positive
options.C: if and only if ∀u ∈ V, f(u) is negative
